heading: Annual reports to Congress by Bureau
label: 1613 Annual reports to Congress by Bureau
text: Each year the Bureau shall make a report to the Congress concerning the administration of its functions under this subchapter, including such recommendations as the Bureau deems necessary or appropriate. In addition, each report of the Bureau shall include its assessment of the extent to which compliance with the requirements imposed under this subchapter is being achieved.
notes: Amendments2010— substituted “Bureau” for “Board” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 111–2031982— struck out requirement that the Attorney General make a report on the same terms as the Board.Pub. L. 97–3751980— substituted “Each year” for “Not later than January 3 of each year after 1969,”.Pub. L. 96–221
